title: The Century-Long Duel
description: On pages 1–4, the century-long duel as the giants’ proud battle becomes the center of the island as the Straw Hats learn what Elbaf honor means.
scene: The Century-Long Duel. The giants’ proud battle becomes the center of the island as the Straw Hats learn what Elbaf honor means. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: W-what!? What's going on? The hell are you doing, Luffy!? Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Let go of me! What's the big idea!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: good, now get on. We're ready to leave at any moment now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: We don't have the time to go look for him!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: But I can't just leave him here...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: She says her duck is missing and won't come even after whistling!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: He got on the boat even before me.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Turn the boat around to head down this river and we should be able to reach the ocean!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: We have to leave as quickly as possible!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Hey, so how many guys are gonna come after us?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I can't say for sure. There's reportedly 2000 people working for Baroque works,

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And towns like whiskey peak aren't uncommon either...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: You mean we could have a 1000 people after us!?

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's possible considering how serious knowing the boss' real identity is.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on the century-long duel. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
